extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL won the 35km flat time trial by one second over THOMAS, regaining maglia rosa with a negative split strategy.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ started conservatively then negative-split to limit losses, opposite pacing strategy to typical TT specialists like KÜNG.
KÄMNA went 11 seconds faster than everyone at first check but started three minutes after ROGLIČ, revealing huge early-split variance.
GEOGHEGAN HART posted career-best TT by staying ahead at all intermediate checks, suggesting sustained power superior to explosive starts.
EVENEPOEL slowed in final sector but maintained fractional advantage, indicating pacing calculation rather than sustained diesel power.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Regained 45-second GC lead despite slowing late, showing tactical maturity in pacing to defend narrow margins
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Lost by one second but demonstrated unexpected TT form, now just 45 seconds down with mountains remaining
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Negative split strategy limited damage to 47 seconds, unconventional for TT specialists but protected GC position
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: positive: Career-best ITT with consistent splits suggests genuine improvement, not just favorable conditions favoring lighter climbers
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: negative: Fourth at four seconds despite pan-flat parcours suited to power merchants reveals declining TT dominance
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: Massive 11-second advantage at first check shows explosive diesel power, though couldn't sustain to finish
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: Mr consistent but never top of charts suggests ceiling as GC threat in TT-heavy Grand Tours
